member.name: Seamus Logan
member.role: MP for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East

addresses: Unit 21, Fraserburgh Business Centre, South Harbour Road, Fraserburgh, Aberdeenshire, AB43 9TN

member.synopsis: Seamus Logan is the Scottish National Party MP for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the roles of Shadow SNP Spokesperson (Environment, Food and Rural Affairs), and Shadow SNP Spokesperson (Health and Social Care).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Office (Accountability) Bill
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether Report Stage and other future stages of the Public Office (Accountability) Bill will take place before the Summer Recess.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The scheduling of Business is a matter for the Business Managers and announced two weeks in advance. Scheduling has not concluded ahead of the Summer Recess, and will be announced in due course.Any scheduling of Commons Remaining Stages of the Public Offi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Experiments: EU Action
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her policies of the roadmap towards phasing out animal testing published by the European Commission.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is aware of the European Commission’s roadmap on phasing out animal testing and is considering its potential implications as part of our broader international engagement on alternative methods. We continue to monitor developments in the EU ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, with reference to the contribution by the Minister for Security of Column 160 during the National Security Ministerial Statement on 14 May 2026, when the Minister will write to the Member for Aberdeen
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Hon. Member received a response on 10 June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Miscarriage: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has made an assessment of the potential implications for his policies of the Miscarriage Patient Charter, including Tommy’s Graded Model of Miscarriage Care, which is being implemented i
writtenQuestions.answerText: Miscarriage can have a devastating impact on women and their families, and we are determined that they receive the support they need.My Rt Hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, has not made a formal assessment of the Miscarriage ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Members: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, when she plans to respond to correspondence from the hon. Member for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East passed to his office on 2 February 2026 with reference numbers SL05232 and PO2026/03476.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A response is being prepared and will be provided in due course. The Department apologises for this delay and regrets that this falls short of expected standards.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to his oral contribution in response to the hon. Member for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East during the Oral Statement of 14 April 2026 on Infected Blood Compensation Scheme, Official Report, co
writtenQuestions.answerText: I can confirm that I wrote to the relevant Minister regarding funding for advocacy charities. The letter to the Member for Aberdeenshire North and Moray East has also now been issued.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Forests
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what safeguards will apply to protect smallholder farmers’ ownership and control of geolocation data collected for compliance with the Forest Risk Commodities regime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the urgency of taking action to ensure that UK consumption of forest risk commodities is not driving deforestation. We will set out our approach to addressing UK consumption of forest risk commodities as soon as possible. We unde...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Forests
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to ensure that deforestation measures do not (a) exclude smallholder farmers from supply chains and (b) increase compliance costs for those farmers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the urgency of taking action to ensure that UK consumption of forest risk commodities is not driving deforestation. We will set out our approach to addressing UK consumption of forest risk commodities as soon as possible. We unde...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Jonathan Ashworth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Prime Minister has made an assessment of the potential merits of appointing Jonathan Ashworth to a head of mission role or the House of Lords.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It would not be appropriate to comment on the hypothetical appointment of individuals.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the potential economic impact of rolling out ultrafast full fibre broadband across Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Project Gigabit is supporting connectivity improvements across Scotland through contracted delivery. Openreach has been awarded a £157 million contract, and a further £131 million of contracts have been signed since the beginning of 2025, together coverin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Menopause: Resignations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking to reduce the number of women leaving the workforce due to menopause-related health issues.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to tackling the challenges women face in the labour market at every stage of life, and with the right menopause workplace support, we can help unlock the full talent and creativity of women across the country. As part of the Em...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Menopause
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what plans she has to (a) monitor and (b) assess the effectiveness of voluntary Menopause Action Plans before they become mandatory in 2027.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Introducing action plans on a voluntary basis is an initial step to provide employers with an opportunity to familiarise themselves with the process before they become mandatory in 2027.Officials will be monitoring the implementation during the voluntary ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: National Wealth Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, when she plans to introduce the National Wealth Fund Bill.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Wealth Fund already has the remit it needs to invest in a wide range of projects, businesses, and assets to drive growth across the UK. HMG has provided NWF with a total capitalisation of £27.8bn and has invested £5.8bn since its launch in 20...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury
